name: Fairfax Financial
confidenceReason: Consistent 15%+ ROE, massive hidden fair value, and disciplined underwriting.
bullCase: The purest modern execution of the Berkshire model. A high-yield fixed-income portfolio combined with disciplined underwriting generates massive, predictable cash flows. Massive 'hidden' fair value on the balance sheet makes the stock intrinsically cheaper than it appears.
bearCase: Susceptibility to catastrophic 'fat-tail' insurance events and volatility in global bond markets which can temporarily impair book value.

cagrModel.basis: Reflects strong underwriting performance and high-yield investment tailwinds.

screener.note: Cheap (0.86) but base return 14% misses the 15% hurdle (CYCLICAL EPS)

description: Fairfax Financial Holdings Limited, through its subsidiaries, provides property and casualty insurance and reinsurance, and investment management services in Canada, the United States, the Middle E...
